acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issue detail output includes namespace line
- [ ] Issue lists include namespace indicator
- [ ] Formatting is deterministic and covered by tests
description:
**Problem**
Human CLI output (show/list/ready/blocked/stale) does not display namespace, which is critical when multiple namespaces are in play.

**Design**
Extend render helpers in src/cli/render.rs to show `namespace` for issue detail and list rows. Keep formatting concise and deterministic.

**Files**
- src/cli/render.rs
title: Show namespace in CLI issue outputs

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI/integration tests no longer import daemon internals directly
- [ ] Daemon-internal tests live under daemon-owned boundaries
- [ ] Critical integration suites still pass
description:
**Problem**
Integration suites still import `beads_rs::daemon::*`, which will break crate extraction.
design: Move tests/fixtures to surface/daemon-core boundaries and keep daemon-internal coverage in daemon-owned test modules.
title: Migrate integration tests/fixtures off daemon-internal imports

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Encode/decode roundtrip tests for each replication message type.
- [ ] VerifiedEvent typestate prevents forwarding events with deferred prev checks.
- [ ] Oversized frames and batches are rejected deterministically with correct error codes.
- [ ] Error frames serialize to the same schema as IPC ErrorPayload.
description:
**Problem**
Replication needs a typed, verifiable on wire protocol. Today there are no CBOR frame types or verification state; without a typestate pipeline, callers can accidentally skip hash or prev link checks and still treat events as valid.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1-9.6 (frame definitions and rules), §0.6 (hashing over EventBody bytes)
- REALTIME_ERRORS.md (error payload shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (EventBytes, EventFrameV1, VerifiedEventAny)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/mod.rs (new), src/core/event.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define CBOR envelope `{ v, type, body }` and per-message bodies (HELLO, WELCOME, EVENTS, ACK, WANT, ERROR) with spec field names.
- Introduce EventFrameV1 that carries EventId, sha256, prev_sha256, and EventBytes<Opaque>.
- Add verification pipeline types: VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified> vs VerifiedEvent<PrevDeferred> (or equivalent) so callers cannot treat a deferred prev check as fully verified.
- Enforce MAX_FRAME_BYTES, MAX_EVENT_BATCH_BYTES, and decoding limits before allocating large buffers; reject oversize frames with ERROR(code="frame_too_large").
- Keep ErrorPayload consistent with IPC; ERROR frames use the same schema and codes.
title: Phase 5: Replication protocol types + framing

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The origin-configured local-only `--no-push` rewrite path asserts the remote store ref is unchanged or still missing after migration.
- [ ] At least one remote-parented migration path asserts preserved `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp` in migrated `meta.json`.
- [ ] The new coverage passes in both default and migration slow-test suites as applicable.
closed_reason: No-push origin isolation and remote-parented metadata preservation are now covered.
description:
**Problem**
The second adversarial review still found two medium coverage gaps. The common local-legacy / origin-configured / remote-store-missing `--no-push` path does not assert that origin stays untouched, and metadata-preservation assertions are still local-only rather than remote-parented.

**Files**: crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs
design:
**Design**
Extend the existing `--no-push` local rewrite test to assert the bare remote still lacks a store ref after migration. Add one remote-parented migration test that rewrites a remote fixture with explicit meta `root_slug` / `last_write_stamp`, migrates from a local clone, and asserts the rewritten local store preserves those meta fields.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 1ac791c3: the migration suite now asserts origin isolation for the common origin-configured local-only `--no-push` rewrite path, adds a remote-parented metadata preservation check for `root_slug` and `last_write_stamp`, and extends the unrelated-divergence slow test to prove dry-run refusal, forced dry-run non-mutation, and no-push origin non-mutation.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all
- just dylint
- cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture
- cargo test --features slow-tests (still fails only on the pre-existing SUN_LEN REPL harness bug tracked by beads-rs-iymc)

title: Migration follow-up: finish no-push origin-isolation and remote-parent metadata coverage

closed_reason: Metrics tests now have a deterministic test-only scope/reset API without changing production behavior.
description:
**Problem**
Metrics snapshot state is process-global, which makes strict counter/histogram assertions in concurrently running tests brittle.

**Design**
Add a test-only metrics reset/scope helper so unit tests can assert exact deltas deterministically without depending on process-global accumulation.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test-only reset/scope API available under cfg(test)
- [ ] Existing sync/daemon metrics tests use deterministic local baselines
- [ ] No production behavior changes

**Files:** crates/beads-daemon/src/metrics.rs, crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
The process-global metrics state now has a test-only scope helper that snapshots and resets both sink and state for deterministic local assertions.

Landed on the current stack:
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/metrics.rs` now provides `TestMetricsScope` under `cfg(test)`
- the scope serializes metrics tests, resets the global metrics state/sink on entry, and restores the previous state on drop
- the metrics unit tests now run inside that scope instead of inheriting ambient process-global emissions

Fresh verification from `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs`:
- `cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib --all-features metrics::tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo nextest run -p beads-daemon --all-features metrics::tests:: --status-level none --final-status-level slow`
- `cargo check -p beads-daemon --lib`
- `cargo xtest --no-fail-fast --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `52.176s`

Note: the plain `cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib` gating mismatch in `testkit/e2e.rs` is tracked separately in `beads-rs-fblt.14`.

title: Provide test-only metrics reset/scope API to avoid cross-test interference

acceptance_criteria: - repo_has_branch returns BranchPresence enum\n- call sites updated\n- cargo check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test pass
description: tests/integration/fixtures/git.rs returns bool for branch presence. Replace with a BranchPresence enum to avoid bool ambiguity and update call sites.
title: Replace repo_has_branch bool with enum

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No duplicate hard-coded record header length remains.
- [ ] `estimated_record_bytes` uses the shared record header size.
description:
**Problem**
`mutation_engine.rs` defines `RECORD_HEADER_BASE_LEN = 88`, duplicating the canonical constant in `wal/record.rs`. If the WAL header layout changes, `estimated_record_bytes` will silently become wrong, causing brittle sizing logic.

**Files**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs
design:
**Design**
- Expose a shared constant or accessor from `wal/record.rs` (e.g., `RecordHeader::base_len()` or `RECORD_HEADER_BASE_LEN` as `pub(crate)`).
- Replace the magic number in `mutation_engine.rs` with the shared value.
title: Deduplicate WAL record header base length constant

description:
**Problem**
In `src/git/collision.rs`, collision resolution remaps the loser and writes a tombstone for the old ID using a "resolution stamp". But the merge rule in `core/state.rs` is: tombstone wins unless the beads `updated_stamp()` is strictly newer.

If the tombstone stamp is newer than the winner beads updated stamp (likely, since resolution stamp is generated "now"), **the winner gets deleted during join**. This violates spec §4.1.1 ("winner retains original id") and is catastrophic.

**Design Options**

*Option A (cleanest):* Lineage-scoped tombstones
Tombstone includes the losing beads `created` stamp (or content-hash) so it only kills that lineage, not the winner occupying the ID.

*Option B (minimal change):* Emit two stamps on collision resolution
- `t_del` for the loser-id tombstone  
- `t_win` for a no-op "bump" on the winner bead so `t_win > t_del`
This makes the winner survive the delete rule while preventing loser resurrection.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Deterministic test: collision where winner is older, loser is newer, resolution happens later
- [ ] Winner survives resolution in all timestamp orderings
- [ ] Loser cannot resurrect after resolution
- [ ] Spec compliance verified

**Files:** src/git/collision.rs, src/core/state.rs
title: Fix collision resolution: winner can be deleted by resolution tombstone
